title: Repository Management
description: Adding, removing, and interacting with package repositories (Packagist, VCS, path, etc.)

actors.name: Developer
actors.description: User invoking composer CLI to manage repositories

actors.name: Packagist
actors.description: Default Composer HTTP repository at packagist.org

actors.name: VCS Host
actors.description: Remote VCS server (GitHub, GitLab, Bitbucket, Git, SVN, Hg, etc.)

use_cases.name: List Repositories
use_cases.description: Reads composer.json via config, outputs all configured repos with type/url. Detects packagist.org presence.

use_cases.name: Add Repository
use_cases.trigger: composer repo add <name> <type> <url>  OR  composer repo add <name> '{...json...}'
use_cases.description: Parses type+url or raw JSON config, then calls configSource->addRepository() or insertRepository() for ordered insertion (--before/--after). Writes to composer.json.

use_cases.name: Remove Repository
use_cases.description: Calls configSource->removeRepository(name). Special case: removing packagist/packagist.org also adds a false-flag entry to explicitly disable it.

use_cases.name: Set Repository URL
use_cases.description: Updates the URL of an existing named repository in composer.json.

use_cases.name: Get Repository URL
use_cases.description: Looks up repo by key or by 'name' field and outputs its URL.

use_cases.name: Disable Packagist
use_cases.description: Adds a false-flag entry for packagist.org, effectively disabling the default Packagist repository.

use_cases.name: Enable Packagist
use_cases.description: Removes the packagist.org false-flag override, restoring the default Packagist repository.

use_cases.name: Register Repository Type
use_cases.description: Maps repository type strings to concrete implementation classes on RepositoryManager.

use_cases.name: Create Repository Instance
use_cases.description: Instantiates a concrete repo from type+config. Wraps in FilterRepository if only/exclude/canonical options present.
use_cases.method: RepositoryManager::createRepository(type, config, name) → new $class(config, io, ...) → optionally new FilterRepository(repo, filterConfig)

use_cases.name: Discover Packages from VCS
use_cases.description: Connects to VCS via driver (GitHubDriver, GitLabDriver, GitDriver, SvnDriver, etc.), reads tags and branches, loads composer.json for each ref, builds package list.

use_cases.name: Find Package in Repositories
use_cases.description: Iterates all registered repositories to find a package matching name+constraint.
methods: RepositoryManager::findPackages(name, constraint) → repository->findPackages() across all repos

use_cases.name: Fetch Packages from Packagist
use_cases.description: ComposerRepository fetches metadata from Packagist HTTP API (packages.json, provider files, metadata v2). Caches responses. Fires PreFileDownloadEvent/PostFileDownloadEvent plugin hooks.

key_relationships: RepositoryCommand depends_on configSource (JsonConfigSource) for write operations
RepositoryManager::createRepository() wraps in FilterRepository when only/exclude/canonical present
VcsRepository composes VcsDriverInterface (driver pattern for git/svn/hg/perforce/fossil)
include_exclude_notes: Only packagist.org can be enabled/disabled via repo command; other repos use add/remove
Ordering: --append adds to end (low priority), default prepends (high priority), --before/--after inserts at specific position
VcsRepository auto-detects driver type if not specified (tries each driver's supports() method)
RepositoryManager::prependRepository() is for plugin use to inject high-priority repos
